import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torchvision.models as models

# Define the ResNet18 model
class ResNet18(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=2):
        super(ResNet18, self).__init__()
        self.resnet = models.resnet18(pretrained=False)
        num_features = self.resnet.fc.in_features
        self.resnet.fc = nn.Linear(num_features, num_classes)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.resnet(x)
        return x

# Creating an instance of ResNet18
model = ResNet18()

    percentage_difference = ((exit_price - entry_price) / entry_price) * 100
    return percentage_difference

Through market research it was determined the following position were required for tree removal and stump grounding.

•	Project Manager
•	Certified Arborist
•	Tree Climber
•	Ground Crew
•	Stump Grinder

Based on the department of Labor’s wage data for Fort Bragg NC. The following was determined to suffice work efficiently.
•	Project Manager: 1 full-time employee. 40 hours/week. $35.29/hour
•	Certified Arborist: 1 full-time employee. 40 hours/week. $25.68/hour
•	Tree Climber: 2 full-time employees. 40 hours/week per employee.  $20.60/hour
•	Ground Crew: 2 full-time employees. 40 hours/week per employee. $15.92/hour
•	Stump Grinder: 1 full-time employee. 40 hours/week. $19.00/hour

*This does not include any additional costs such as equipment rental or disposal fees.

Factors to consider include: 
•	The scope is projected for 50 work orders per year.

•	Tree size and location - The size and location of the tree affect the cost of its removal. A larger tree or one located in a difficult-to-reach area will require more equipment and labor, thus increasing the overall cost.

•	Species of tree - Different tree species have different densities, which impact the time and effort required to remove them. Certain species may also require special equipment, increasing the cost of removal.

•	Stump size and location - Similarly to trees, stump size and location play a significant role in determining the cost. A larger stump or one located in a hard-to-reach spot will require additional effort and equipment, leading to higher costs.

•	Access to the site - The accessibility of the work site also influences costs, as equipment and labor may need to navigate obstacles such as buildings, fences, or uneven terrain, and the cost for this may vary. 

•	Safety considerations - The safety of the workers and the site must be taken into consideration. Depending on the nature of the job or location, additional safety equipment or protocols could be necessary, which can add to the overall cost of the project. 

•	Additional services - If additional services are required or requested, such as hauling away debris or grinding the stump, they will also increase the cost of removal. we also have the following Item Description

Croy (née Upshaw), formerly of Yuba City, died on July 19, 2020 in Eureka, CA at the age of 94.Helen was born June 10, 1926 and grew up in a large family, the third of seven children, in Sapulpa, OK. On November 22, 1949 she married Otis N. Croy in Northern California. She attended Chico State University and earned a teaching credential., and eventually her Master's degree. Her career in kindergarten and 1st grade teaching spanned three decades and included service at Tierra Buena Elementary School in Yuba City, Paradise Elementary School in Paradise, and Brittan Elementary School in Sutter. Helen loved the little ones and was a lifelong reading advocate.After retiring from teaching, Helen enjoyed a variety of volunteer activities including A Woman's Friend Crisis Pregnancy Center, the Pink Ladies Auxiliary, and Volunteers in Policing. At the latter alone she volunteered 12 years and over 4,400 hours.Helen is survived by a son Howard of San Francisco, CA (wife, Kalani Baker), a son Clayton of Oklahoma City, OK (wife, Marty Croy), five grandchildren (Howie, Shiloh, Heather, Sarah, and Celeste), and five great-grandchildren (Jessica, Sophia, India Rose, Georgiana, and Izaiah).Helen was preceded in death by her husband Otis, five brothers, one sister, and one daughter (Naomi Florence).In lieu of flowers, donations may be made to Guideposts (magazine) or A Woman's Friend in Yuba City.Share online condolences atwww.appealdemocrat.com" in json file
